prompt:
Declaration (in markdown format):

# CI/CD Pipeline: backend-api

Trigger: on push to main

## Step 1 (parallel)
- Lint (Go)
- Type check (Go)
- Test (ginkgo, coverage min 80%)
- Security scan (snyk)

## Step 2 (if all four pass)
- Build docker image, push to ghcr.io/lambda-run/backend-api
- Deploy to staging with rolling strategy
- Smoke test (60s timeout)
- If smoke test passes: deploy to prod with canary strategy (10%)

## Step 3 (if any failed)
- Notify #deploys in Slack
- Block the merge

Output a flat bullet list manifest of every concrete element this declaration implies.
manifest:
- CI/CD pipeline named `backend-api`
- Trigger on push
- Trigger branch `main`
- Step 1 executes in parallel
- Step 1 includes Go lint
- Step 1 includes Go type check
- Step 1 includes tests using `ginkgo`
- Step 1 enforces test coverage minimum of 80%
- Step 1 includes security scan using `snyk`
- Step 2 runs only if Go lint passes
- Step 2 runs only if Go type check passes
- Step 2 runs only if `ginkgo` tests pass
- Step 2 runs only if `snyk` security scan passes
- Step 2 builds a Docker image
- Step 2 pushes the Docker image to `ghcr.io/lambda-run/backend-api`
- Step 2 deploys to staging
- Staging deployment uses a rolling strategy
- Step 2 runs a smoke test
- Smoke test timeout is 60 seconds
- Production deployment runs only if smoke test passes
- Production deployment uses a canary strategy
- Production canary percentage is 10%
- Step 3 runs if Go lint fails
- Step 3 runs if Go type check fails
- Step 3 runs if `ginkgo` tests fail
- Step 3 runs if test coverage is below 80%
- Step 3 runs if `snyk` security scan fails
- Step 3 notifies Slack channel `#deploys`
- Step 3 blocks the merge
